pathwayTagline: Protect → observe → move → restore → clean → feed → eliminate

sections.title: Infection Control
sections.rationale: Every clinical skill begins and ends with infection control. Students must internalize this as muscle memory before touching a patient.

sections.title: Measurement and Recording
sections.rationale: Observation-only skills with no physical intervention. They train precision and documentation before students are trusted to act.

sections.title: Mobility and Positioning
sections.rationale: Before personal care, students must move patients safely. Positioning and transfers prevent injury during bathing, dressing, and elimination.

sections.title: Restorative and Support
sections.rationale: Once mobility is established, students learn to maintain and restore function — preventing deterioration, not just assisting movement.

sections.title: Personal Care
sections.rationale: The core of daily CNA work. Mobility skills are prerequisites for bathing, grooming, and dressing with dignity.

sections.title: Feeding and Nutrition
sections.rationale: Feeding follows hygiene and positioning. It is cognitively distinct — swallowing observation, aspiration risk, and dignity at the table.

sections.title: Elimination and Peri Care
sections.rationale: The most intimate care. Full confidence in infection control, mobility, and personal care comes first — then elimination.

skills.title: Hand Hygiene (Hand Washing)
skills.pedagogicalReason: The single universal act. Teaches the before-and-after mindset — nothing happens without it.

steps.note: Avoid splashing on uniform; keep forearms and wrists lower than elbows.

subSteps: Rub hands palm to palm
Rub back of right and left hand (fingers interlaced)
Rub palm to palm with fingers interlaced
Perform rotational rubbing of left and right thumbs
Rub fingertips against palm of opposite hand
Rub wrists
Repeat sequence at least two times

steps.note: Credentia absorbs this into Steps 5 & 7 — GWC scores it as a standalone observable action. Students must demonstrate it explicitly.

subSteps: Rub fingertips against palm of opposite hand

steps.note: GWC: paper towel only. Credentia also allows knee/foot control — GWC governs on exam.

steps.text: Never lean on or touch sink

skills.title: Donning and Removing PPE (Gown and Gloves)
skills.pedagogicalReason: Builds on hand hygiene: wash before donning and after removing. The sequence is the lesson.

steps.note: Use clean, non-sterile gloves for isolation care.
Sequence matters: gloves off before gown, before leaving the room — evaluator watches for this order.

subSteps: Grasp outside of first glove at palm; peel off
Slide fingers of bare hand under cuff of remaining glove at wrist; peel off turning inside out

steps.note: ⚠️ VIDEO WARNING: This step is missing from the reference video — video performs hand hygiene only after gown disposal. GWC requires it HERE, between glove removal and gown unfastening.

subSteps: Start at shoulders; pull down
Turn inside out as you remove
Fold soiled area to soiled area
Hold away from body; do not touch floor

skills.title: Counts and Records Radial Pulse
skills.pedagogicalReason: Simplest vital sign — one finger location, one count, one unit (bpm). Introduces the count-then-record loop.

steps.note: Start on a whole number on the watch, then count

skills.title: Counts and Records Respirations
skills.pedagogicalReason: Taught immediately after pulse because they share a 60-second window. Observe without alerting the patient.

steps.note: Say "for testing purposes." In real practice, never tell the patient you're counting respirations — it changes the rate.
Start on a whole number on the watch, then count.

skills.title: Measures and Records Manual Blood Pressure
skills.pedagogicalReason: Most complex vital sign — equipment, two values, and interpretation. Builds on pulse and respiration rhythm.

steps.text: Position patient; arm at heart level
steps.note: Ask which arm the patient prefers.
Lower cuff margin sits 1″ above the elbow bend.
If a beat is heard immediately on deflation, fully deflate, wait 1 min, reinflate to no more than 200.
Both numbers must each be within ±8 mmHg.

skills.title: Measures and Records Weight of Ambulatory Client
skills.pedagogicalReason: Requires the patient to be upright and cooperative. Bridges measurement toward mobility.

steps.note: Tolerance: record weight within ±2 lb / 0.9 kg of actual.

skills.title: Measures and Records Urinary Output
skills.pedagogicalReason: The only measurement tied to a bodily output. Bridge skill into the elimination section.

steps.note: Tolerance: record urine volume within ±25 mL of actual.

skills.title: Positions on Side
skills.pedagogicalReason: Bed-based, lowest-risk movement. No equipment. Teaches body mechanics, alignment, and patient comfort.

steps.text: Raise far side rail for safety
Move to working side of bed
Explain move toward you on three
Count three; move patient toward you
Instruct patient to move near arm
Raise patient's near knee to assist
Explain turning toward rail on three
Count three; turn toward side rail
Guard face from rail and pillow
Check patient not on bottom arm
Check body alignment from bed end
Pillow under top arm; protect ribs
Pillow under top knee; protect ankles

skills.title: Transfers from Bed to Wheelchair Using Transfer Belt
skills.pedagogicalReason: The patient leaves the bed. Requires belt, coordination, and two-point communication.

steps.text: Remove wheelchair foot pedals if needed
Assist patient to sit; dangle feet
Position wheelchair; strong side toward it
Count three; assist patient to stand
Instruct patient to grasp wheelchair arms
Assist patient to seated in wheelchair

skills.title: Assists to Ambulate Using Transfer Belt
skills.pedagogicalReason: Most demanding mobility skill — patient fully upright and moving. Gait, balance, and fall prevention all apply.

steps.text: Move to weak side; hold belt
Assist back to bed; remove belt
Call light; bed low and locked

skills.title: Performs Modified PROM for One Shoulder
skills.pedagogicalReason: Upper body first. Larger joint, more intuitive range of motion. Introduces PROM mechanics.

skills.title: Performs Modified PROM for One Knee and One Ankle
skills.pedagogicalReason: Lower body, two joints. More complex; builds on the shoulder movement pattern already learned.
skills.rtcVideoTitle: 16 Passive Range of Motion One Knee

subSteps: Keeping the patient's leg straight, gently move their entire leg away from their
body (i.e., abduction).
Move their legs gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Ask the patient if they are experiencing any pain during movement.
Stop the ROM movement if the patient reports pain or displays objective signs
of pain.
Keeping the patient's leg straight, move their entire leg toward their body (i.e.,
adduction).
Complete abduction and adduction movements of the hip according to the
order in their restorative care plan.
Continue to correctly support joints by keeping one hand under the patient's
knee and the other hand under the patient's ankle.
Bend the patient's knee and hip up toward the patient's trunk (i.e., flexion of
hip and knee at the same time).
Move the patient's leg gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Ask the patient if they are experiencing any pain during movement.
Stop the ROM movement if the patient reports pain or displays objective signs
of pain.
Straighten their knee and hip (i.e., extension of knee and hip at the same time).
Complete flexion and extension movements of the knee and hip according to
the order in the restorative care plan.

skills.title: Applies One Knee-High Elastic Stocking
skills.pedagogicalReason: Applied after PROM — supports the same lower-extremity circulation PROM exercises protect. Same goal, different tool.

steps.text: Turn stocking inside out to heel
Place stocking over toes and heel
Call light; bed low and locked

skills.title: Gives Modified Bed Bath (Face and One Arm, Hand and Underarm)
skills.pedagogicalReason: Largest task — the body itself. Sets the standard for privacy, temperature, and patient dignity.

steps.text: Wash hands, fingers, fingernails; pat dry

skills.title: Provides Mouth Care
skills.pedagogicalReason: Moves to the face and head after the bath. Natural clinical sequence — mouth care follows or accompanies bath.

steps.text: Place all supplies on a barrier
Remove towel; place in linen bag

skills.title: Cleans Upper or Lower Denture
skills.pedagogicalReason: Extension of mouth care — same skill with an added device. Follows directly.

steps.text: Place all supplies on a barrier

skills.title: Provides Foot Care on One Foot
skills.pedagogicalReason: Bottom of the body. Mirrors the lower-extremity focus from PROM. Introduces skin integrity inspection.

skills.title: Dresses Client with Affected (Weak) Right Arm
skills.pedagogicalReason: Last in personal care — requires positioning, affected-side awareness, and sequencing. Most cognitively complex personal care skill.

steps.text: Remove gown from left side first

steps.note: Affected side dressed first; unaffected side last.

skills.title: Feeds Client Who Cannot Feed Self
skills.pedagogicalReason: Patient must be clean, dressed, and upright. Mirrors the care shift routine — hygiene before feeding.

steps.text: Ask about mouth care before eating
Assist patient to clean their hands
Sit at eye level facing patient
Offer small amounts at reasonable rate
Allow time to chew and swallow
Alternate foods and fluids until full
Ask about mouth care after eating

skills.title: Assists With Use of Bedpan
skills.pedagogicalReason: Most basic elimination assist — no indwelling device. Teaches privacy, positioning, and cleanup sequence.

steps.text: Turn patient; place barrier under buttocks

skills.title: Provides Perineal Care (Peri-Care) for Female
skills.pedagogicalReason: Post-elimination hygiene step. Completes the loop: assist, clean, document.

steps.text: Clean other labia side; clean portion
Clean perineal area top to bottom
Put first washcloth in linen bag
Rinse other labia side; clean portion
Rinse perineal area top to bottom
Put second washcloth in linen bag
Turn patient; grasp raised side rail
Apply water and soap; third washcloth
Clean one buttock side; wipe away
Clean other buttock side; wipe away
Put third washcloth in linen bag
Rinse one buttock side; wipe away
Rinse other buttock side; wipe away
Put fourth washcloth in linen bag

skills.title: Provides Catheter Care for Female
skills.pedagogicalReason: Last skill in the entire list. Requires infection control, perineal anatomy, and documentation. Most invasive and highest-consequence skill.

steps.text: Use clean washcloth portion each stroke
Put first washcloth in linen bag
Put second washcloth in linen bag
